description: The Elephant and Castle Mob was an independent street gang active in London's underworld during the interwar years. They were rivals of Charles Sabini and the Birmingham Boys, and were eventually forced out by Sabini with Sicilian mafiosi. The gang achieved dominance on race courses and in London's West End from the 1910s to the 1930s, led by the McDonald brothers. They disappeared by the end of World War II.
